title: senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. cossyrensis
intro: Senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. cossyrensis, also known as the Sardinian ragwort, is a plant species within the Senecio genus, characterized by its unique yellow flowers and daisy-like appearance. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, containing compounds such as alkaloids and sesquiterpenes, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. cossyrensis include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
